corpus.single_cell_source: Mojtahedi et al. 2016 Table S2 (three lineages)

question: Can the grid modal-growth + fit-quality-gate moat be posed on the DNB critical-slowing-down early-warning corpora?
verdict: The grid modal-growth moat cannot be posed on DNB early warning: its rising limbs are 4 points at most — below the 6 a growth-form fit and gate need — so the fit-quality gate is uninformative (R² 0.67–0.86, all 4/4 kept, none rejected) and the exponential growth rate re-orders the transitions exactly as the existing linear slope does, adding no separating information. The DNB rise is a genuine critical-slowing-down divergence, not the exponential instability the moat is built to fit.
